---
title: Quantifying metacognitive performance with the `hmetad` package in `R`
author: 
  name: Kevin O'Neill
  affiliation: 
    name: University College London
    department: Department of Experimental Psychology
  degrees: PhD
  orcid: 0000-0001-7401-9802
  url: https://kevingoneill.github.io
bibliography: citations.bib
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ucl.scss]
    resources:
      - "fonts/*.otf"
#    embed-resources: true
    show-notes: false
    slide-number: false
    pdf-separate-fragments: true
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
# enable tikz figures
filters:
  - tikz
tikz:
  cache: false
  save-tex: true
  tex-dir: tikz-tex   # any path; defaults to 'tikz-tex'
  svg-command: "pdf2svg {input} {output}"  # use pdf2svg because inkscape breaks fonts
# R code execution
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    dev: svg
    dev.args: { bg: "transparent" }
---


# What is metacognition? { .dark }


## Type 1 and type 2 decisions
```{.tikz}
%%| filename: type1type2
%%| caption: This is a test.
%%| additionalPackages: \usepackage{cmbright}
\include{tikz_header.tex}
\begin{tikzpicture}
	\node[image] (stimulus) { \includegraphics[width=1cm]{figures/gabor.png} };
	\node (type1) [right = of stimulus] {
		Direction? \\ 
		L \hspace{.25em} R
	};
	\node (type2) [right = of type1] { 
		Confidence? \\
		1 \hspace{.1em} 2 \hspace{.1em} 3 \hspace{.1em} 4 
	};
	\path (stimulus) edge (type1);
	\path (type1) edge (type2);
\end{tikzpicture}
```

## Type 1 and type 2 decisions

::::{.fragment}
```{dot}
//| fig-align: center
graph {
	graph [fontname = "UCLSans" fillcolor = "#361a54"];
    node [fontname = "UCLSans" style=filled, fillcolor = "#361a54" color = "#361a54" fontcolor = "#fafafa"];
    edge [fontname = "UCLSans" color = "#361a54"];
	bgcolor="transparent";
	A -- B
}
```
::::






## Type 1 and type 2 decisions
 
:::: {.columns }

:::: {.fragment .appear .column width="50%"}
### Type 1

Decision about the world

:::: {.incremental .much-smaller}
- Is there a stimulus present?
- Are the dots moving left or right?
- Have I seen this image before?
::::
::::

:::: {.fragment .column width="50%"}
### Type 2

Decision about yourself
 
:::: {.incremental .much-smaller}
- Was my type 1 decision correct?
::::

::::

::::

::: { .ref .right .bottom}
@clarke1959
:::





## Hierarchies of metacognition

Progressively more abstract/global self-estimates

![](https://ars.els-cdn.com/content/image/1-s2.0-S0006322321013299-gr3_lrg.jpg){.center-horizontally}

## References
